items.q: When energy is 'lost' as heat due to friction, what has really happened?
items.correct: Energy has been transferred to the thermal store of the surroundings — it's not destroyed, just dissipated.
items.misconception: Energy has been used up and no longer exists.
items.random_wrong: Energy has been converted into mass following E=mc^2.
items.why: Everyday language ('used up', 'lost') and bills ('energy used') reinforce the idea energy is consumed, contradicting conservation.

items.q: If a car doubles its speed, what happens to its kinetic energy?
items.correct: It quadruples (because KE = 1/2 mv^2 — kinetic energy is proportional to v^2).
items.misconception: Kinetic energy doubles because energy is proportional to speed.
items.random_wrong: Kinetic energy stays the same because mass hasn't changed.
items.why: Students assume linear relationships (double the input, double the output) and forget the v^2 factor in the kinetic energy formula.

items.q: Is the internal energy of a system the same as the heat it contains?
items.correct: No — a system doesn't 'contain heat'; internal energy is the total kinetic and potential energy of its particles, changed by heating or doing work.
items.misconception: Yes — internal energy is just the stored heat in the object.
items.random_wrong: No — internal energy refers only to the chemical potential energy.
items.why: Students treat heat as a substance stored in objects rather than a process of energy transfer, blurring the heat/internal energy distinction.

items.q: Total energy of an SHM oscillator is proportional to:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A². “1/A” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Resonance is maximum when driver frequency equals:
items.why: Correct answer: Natural f. “Twice natural f” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Spring constant 200 N/m, amplitude 0.1 m. Total energy (J)?
items.why: The real figure is 1.0. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
